description:
You are working as a backup administrator.  
  Inside the path  
  /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/  
  there is an existing, **already-created** plain-text file named  

  /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/backup_manifest_20230510.txt  

  The file has **exactly six lines**, laid out as follows (including the comment line on top):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │# Backup Manifest generated 2023-05-10                                       │
  │/etc/passwd:2048                                                             │
  │/etc/group:1024                                                              │
  │/var/log/syslog:512000                                                       │
  │/home/user/data.txt:4096                                                     │
  │/usr/local/bin/script.sh:8192                                               │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Task:
  1. Using a single terminal command that employs either **awk** or **sed** (no temporary helper files), create a new file named  

     /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/backup_manifest_20230510.csv  

  2. The CSV file must have **exactly six lines** and follow this strict format so that automated tests can verify it:

     • Line-1 is a header line that must read literally  
       file_path,file_size_bytes

     • Lines 2-6 must list the same five backups that appear in the txt file, in the **same order**, converted so the colon is replaced by a single comma.  
       Example pattern (do not wrap):  
       /etc/passwd,2048

  3. Make sure the new file permissions stay as the default created by your shell (i.e., agent does not need sudo or manual chmod).

  Acceptance checklist the grader will follow:
     a. The file /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/backup_manifest_20230510.csv exists.  
     b. It contains exactly six newline-terminated lines.  
     c. The first line is the literal header shown above.  
     d. Lines 2-6 match, in order, the five original records with “:” replaced by “,”.  
     e. No extra whitespace, spaces, tabs, or blank lines appear anywhere.  

  Produce only the command output naturally generated by the shell; no extra commentary is needed in the terminal.
truth:

    • File /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/backup_manifest_20230510.txt already exists with the exact six lines (each ending with '\n'):

      # Backup Manifest generated 2023-05-10
      /etc/passwd:2048
      /etc/group:1024
      /var/log/syslog:512000
      /home/user/data.txt:4096
      /usr/local/bin/script.sh:8192

  Desired post-task state:
    • New file /home/user/backups/2023-05-10/backup_manifest_20230510.csv exists containing exactly:

      file_path,file_size_bytes
      /etc/passwd,2048
      /etc/group,1024
      /var/log/syslog,512000
      /home/user/data.txt,4096
      /usr/local/bin/script.sh,8192

      (Each line terminated by a single '\n'; no trailing blank line.)

  The automatic test will verify byte-for-byte equality of this file relative to the block above.
